verdict: AI displacement risk is moderate, with some tasks being automated but still requiring human expertise in others.

reasoning_steps.title: Task automation
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like preparing sketches, installing conduit, and testing electrical systems can be automated to some extent using LLMs.

reasoning_steps.title: Human expertise still required
reasoning_steps.body: However, tasks that require human judgment, such as diagnosing malfunctions, inspecting electrical systems, and ensuring compliance with codes, are less likely to be automated.

adjacent_careers.name: Electrician's Assistant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: This role involves supporting electricians with tasks like material preparation and equipment setup, but still requires human expertise in some areas.

adjacent_careers.name: Electrical Inspector
adjacent_careers.reasoning: This role involves ensuring compliance with electrical codes and regulations, which may require more human judgment than automation can provide.

recent_developments.headline: Hands-on trades remain among the least automated
recent_developments.impact: Physical, in-situ work captures very little AI use today; trades have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.

recent_developments.headline: AI moves into estimating, scheduling, and dispatch
recent_developments.impact: Back-office tasks for skilled-trades businesses are being absorbed by AI assistants, not the field work itself.

recent_developments.headline: AR + AI tools enter the field for diagnostics
recent_developments.impact: Technicians use phone-based vision models to identify parts and walk through repairs in real time.

quotes.quote: We do not have enough plumbers, electricians, or HVAC techs. AI cannot fix a leaking pipe. The shortage in the trades is only going to widen.
quotes.speaker: Mike Rowe
quotes.title: Host of Dirty Jobs; founder, mikeroweWORKS Foundation

quotes.quote: The tasks most exposed to large language models are not physical. Plumbers, electricians, and construction workers have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.
quotes.speaker: Tyna Eloundou and Pamela Mishkin
quotes.title: Researchers, OpenAI
quotes.source: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of LLMs

quotes.quote: The world is short on skilled tradespeople and we have spent two generations telling kids the only path is a four-year degree. AI is now coming for the desk jobs first.
quotes.speaker: Mark Cuban
quotes.title: Investor; co-founder, Cost Plus Drugs
